theme: Second sign-flip family: failed-replication contamination across comma-joined clauses. _split_clauses splits on sentence boundaries and semicolons but NOT commas, and _failed_replication scans the whole clause. So a 'failed to replicate / could not reproduce' statement about an UNRELATED experiment, comma-joined into the same sentence as a strong reversal, marks the reversal event itself as a failed replication -> it is scored as a failed replication that SUPPORTS the scoped prior -> the contradiction RAISES the belief it refutes. The semicolon-separated form is correctly immune, isolating the cause to clause segmentation.

items.body: A defined factor returned fibroblasts to a pluripotent state, though an unrelated control assay failed to replicate.
items.note: The primary result is a strong reversal (contradiction of C3c). The 'failed to replicate' is about a different experiment ('an unrelated control assay'), comma-joined into the same clause. _failed_replication matches it clause-wide, so event.failed_replication=True and _targets scores it as a failed replication that SUPPORTS the scoped prior -> C3c RAISED 0.92 -> 0.947 instead of lowered to 0.43. Sign inversion.

items.body: A defined factor returned fibroblasts to a pluripotent state, whereas a parallel staining protocol could not reproduce.
items.note: Second construction ('could not reproduce', comma-joined via 'whereas'). Same flip to 0.947. Confirms it is general to failed-replication phrasing comma-joined into a reversal clause, not one phrase.

items.body: A defined factor returned fibroblasts to a pluripotent state.
items.note: Control: plain reversal correctly LOWERS C3c to 0.43.

items.body: A defined factor returned fibroblasts to a pluripotent state; a parallel staining protocol failed to replicate.
items.note: Defended: the SAME failed-replication clause separated by a SEMICOLON is split into its own clause, so it does not contaminate the reversal -> C3c correctly lowered to 0.43. Isolates the root cause to comma-blind clause segmentation (and to _failed_replication scanning clause-wide).
